info.title: RAG Management API
info.description: This documentation provides a comprehensive description of the API for managing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. Through the APIs, it is possible to create a RAG, retrieve its details, initiate file indexing, and delete it. It is also possible to view all of a user's RAGs. Furthermore, the APIs allow for document management, enabling the upload of both text documents and images. Additionally, metadata can be specified for each document to filter search queries. The service's three main endpoints are dedicated to querying the RAG. These endpoints allow users to submit queries and receive responses that combine the power of generative models (LLMs) with specific knowledge extracted from the indexed documents.

tags.name: RAG
tags.description: Endpoints related to the lifecycle management of RAG.

tags.name: Documents
tags.description: Endpoints for managing individual documents within a RAG.

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Endpoints dedicated to querying and searching for information within the RAG.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of all RAG for the authenticated user.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: Optional. Number of items to skip for pagination. If not specified, the default value is 0.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Optional. Maximum number of items to return. If not specified, the default value is 100.

post.description: Creates a new, empty RAG for the authenticated user. A maximum of 10 RAGs can be created per user. Should you require more, please reach out to support for assistance.

409.description: Conflict, e.g., a RAG with this ID or name already exists.

422.description: Validation error on the submitted data (e.g., missing name, invalid options).

get.description: Retrieves the full details of a single RAG by its ID.

403.description: The Rag ID does not belong to the authenticated user

422.description: Validation error returned by the API.

patch.description: Starts the indexing process for a specific RAG. This will process any files pending upload or deletion.

requestBody.description: Requires `startIndexing` to be set to `true` to initiate the process.

startIndexing.description: Must be true to start indexing.

404.description: RAG with this identifier not found.

422.description: Validation error returned by the API.

428.description: Precondition Failed. Validation Error, e.g., startIndexing is not `true` or the RAG is not in a 'ready' state.

delete.description: Deletes an entire RAG and all its associated documents. Requires explicit confirmation in the request body.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the RAG to delete.

parameters.name: deleteEntireRag
parameters.description: Set to `true` to confirm the permanent deletion of the RAG and all data within it.

schema.description: Set to `true` to confirm the permanent deletion of the RAG and all data within it.
schema.title: Confirm Full Deletion

message.example: The RAG with ID 68947e1e0dab7e743108abfb and all associated files have been deleted.

400.description: Bad Request, e.g., 'confirm_full_deletion' parameter is missing or false.

404.description: RAG with this identifier not found.

428.description: Precondition Failed. Validation error on the submitted data.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of document within a specific RAG.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: Optional. Number of items to skip for pagination. If not specified, the default value is 0.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Optional. Maximum number of items to return. If not specified, the default value is 100.

404.description: The Rag ID does not exist in the database

post.description: Uploads a file by sending its content as a base64 encoded string in a JSON body.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the RAG where the document will be uploaded.

requestBody.description:
Uploads a document. The server validates the file content. Only the following MIME types are permitted:

* `image/png`
* `application/pdf`
* `text/plain`
* `text/html`
* `application/json`
* `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document` (DOCX)
* `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet` (XLSX)
* `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation` (PPTX)

The server will return a 415 error if the type is not supported.

200.description: Document saved successfully. The file is now pending indexing.

400.description: Bad Request, e.g., invalid JSON metadata provided.

404.description: The Rag ID does not exist in the database.

409.description: File ID already exists or RAG is indexing.

413.description: The document size is too large.

415.description: Unsupported Media Type. The document's MIME type is not allowed.

422.description: Validation error returned by the API.

428.description: Precondition Failed. Validation error on the submitted data (e.g., invalid base64, missing fields).

delete.description: Deletes a single document from a RAG. If the RAG is busy, the file is scheduled for deletion.

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: ID of the file to delete.

message.example: The file with ID 689af360b0ef3bdb09019770 has been deleted

422.description: Validation error returned by the API.

Callback.description: Configuration for event notifications. Can be `false` if not set.

method.description: Indicate through which method you want to receive data with the callback, by default it is POST

field.description: Name of the field containing the callback return data

url.description: Url where the callback call will be made

data.description: Object to be completed at your convenience with additional data that you can use back with the callback

RAGCreate.description: Schema for creating a new RAG.

Document.description: Represents a document's metadata within a RAG.

name.description: The original filename of the document.

state.description: The current state of the document (e.g., 'wait', 'ready', 'to_remove', 'to_index').

sizeByte.description: Size of the document in byte

mimeType.description: The detected MIME type of the document (e.g., 'application/pdf').

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the document was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last update.

paymentDueDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the next storage payment is due.

DocumentListInfo.description: Represents a document's metadata within a RAG.

name.description: The original filename of the document.

state.description: The current state of the document (e.g., 'wait', 'ready', 'to_remove', 'to_index').

sizeByte.description: Size of the document in bytes.

mimeType.description: The detected MIME type of the document (e.g., 'application/pdf').

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the document was created.

paymentDueDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the next storage payment is due.

DocumentUpload.description: Schema for uploading a new document.

fileName.description: The name of the file being uploaded.

fileContent.description: The base64 encoded content of the file.

Metadata.description: A flexible object for custom metadata. Keys must match the defined custom types.

customKeyDate.description: A custom date-time value in ISO 8601 format.

SearchQuery.description: Schema for performing a search query.

ragId.description: The ID of the RAG to perform the search on.

query.description: The text of the search query.

maxLen.description: The maximum number of results to return from the search.

metadataFilter.description:
Allows filtering search results using a simplified syntax.

**1. Available Keys:**
`customKeyStr` (Text)
`customKeyInt` (Integer)
`customKeyDate` (Date)
`customKeyBool` (Boolean)
`customKeyGeoLatitude` (Number)
`customKeyGeoLongitude` (Number)

**2. Logical Operators:**
Use `AND`, `OR`. Group with `()`.

**3. Comparison Operators:**
`=` (Equals)
`!=` (Not equals)
`>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=` (Only for `customKeyInt` and `customKeyDate`)

**4. Values:**
- **Text:** `value` (unquoted) or `"value with spaces"` (quoted).
- **Boolean:** `true` or `false`.
- **Date:** ISO 8601 string (e.g., `2025-10-31T23:59:59Z`).

**5. Negation:**
Use `!=` or the `-` prefix (e.g., `-(customKeyBool = true)`).

**Example:**
`(customKeyInt > 10 AND customKeyStr != "exclude") OR customKeyDate < 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z`

SearchAnswerQuery.description: Performs a search based on a natural language question and specifies the desired format for the answer.

promptStyle.description: The style of prompt to use for generation.

ConversationQuery.description: Extends a standard search query with a session ID for conversational context.

session.description: Session ID from a previous conversational response to maintain context.

promptStyle.description: The style of prompt to use for generation.

snippets.description: A list of relevant text excerpts from the searched documents.
snippets.example: ... service allows you to ascertain in real time whether any car is insured, with which company and any policy expiry date. All via API SIGN UP FREE ...

id.description: The ID of the matching document.

name.description: The name of the matching document.

state.description: The state of the matching document.

SearchResult.description: A list of search results, where each item corresponds to a matching document.

snippets.description: A list of relevant text excerpts from the searched documents
snippets.example: HomeProductsCompany Balance Sheet - Italy DOCUMENT EXAMPLE Company Balance Sheet - Italy...

id.description: The ID of the matching document.

name.description: The name of the matching document.

state.description: The state of the matching document.

SearchAnswer.description: A response containing a generated answer and its source documents.

answer.description: The natural language answer generated by the AI.
answer.example: Per richiedere un bilancio, è necessario il numero di partita IVA o il codice fiscale della società.[0]....

SearchConversation.description: Performs a search query within an existing conversation, using a session ID to maintain the context of previous questions and answers.

session.description: The updated session ID to be used in the next turn of the conversation.
